mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS grows by > 50k in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank policy tightening expectations, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion, creating positive momentum in equity markets that translates to S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO-GDPC1 combined signal falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signal reduced corporate investment and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2% and HOUST < 1000.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) reflects strong household demand for housing, which stimulates related industries and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST increases by > 5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 5% indicates tightening labor markets that push wages higher, increasing corporate costs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business confidence, creating positive momentum in equity markets that translates to S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS-HOUST combined signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals supply-side shocks that reduce corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105 and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.3%.
